description: Golden QA set for RAG evaluation, grounded in the CURRENT pgvector store after the 2026-06-07 medium-model re-embed. student_id values are the roll numbers used as the store key. Cases were verified end-to-end (retrieval + Groq) against the live DB. Mix of self-referential (own spoken/chat, tight top_k), wrong-subject refusal, GENERAL class-content questions (non-self-referential, unfiltered so full class context is retrievable), and — CONCEPT/'related' questions that ground on the authoritative 'material' chunks from materials ingestion. NOTE ON THE CONCEPT CASES: any case with expected_chunk_types=['material'] stays RED until operators ingest that class's materials (scripts.ingest_materials) into the live store, then GREEN; its required_concept_groups are relationship-level and grounded in determinants already verified in class_context — reconcile the concept groups + evidence_quotes to the real ingested slide/notes text before relying on the case, without loosening the prompt/top_k just to force a pass. EXTENDED 2026-07-16: +7 English cases (5 concept incl. material-grounded, 2 refusals incl. the Macbeth near-miss trap) drafted from the post-English-embed 4,494-row store; every evidence_quote verbatim-verified against stored chunk text at draft time; quote-verified.
generated_from: scripts/run_pipeline.py --skip-transcribe --model medium with rosters C1 (Economics) / C3 (Math)
general-question expected concepts derived from live retrieved class_context (2026-07-03), not invented
concept cases target the 'material' chunks from scripts.ingest_materials; operators reconcile material-grounded expectations against the ingested slide/notes text

cases.question: What did I say or ask during class?
evidence_quotes: First I was getting 25 but now I am getting 20/11
Nisha , do we have to send our answers ?
cases.notes: Self-referential phrasing scopes retrieval to the student's own spoken+chat chunks. Saisha mostly typed in chat; this confirms quiet-but-typing students still surface their own contributions.

cases.question: What did I say about the Balance of Payments?
cases.notes: Balance of Payments is an Economics topic; Saisha is in Math. The bot must decline rather than borrow another class's content. Validates safe refusal + per-student isolation. (No trust-ack required: a clean wrong-subject refusal has no low-confidence flags to cite.)

cases.question: What did I say about the intercept in the supply function?
evidence_quotes: Yeah, the intercept is it should be negative.
cases.notes: Bhagyashree's own spoken contribution on the supply-function intercept (medium transcript). Exercises a rich-content student's spoken chunks.

cases.question: What did I say about quantity supplied being negative at low prices?
evidence_quotes: Quantity supply is negative 12
cases.notes: Confirms the medium transcript captured Bhagyashree's worked example of the supply schedule at low prices.

cases.question: What numbers did I work out in class?
evidence_quotes: Initial Number of girls- 12 new no. of girls- 18
cases.notes: Natural contribution phrasing ('what numbers did I work out') beyond the canonical 'what did I say'. Exercises the broadened self-referential matcher: Swarnima only typed in chat, so this must scope to spoken+chat and surface her numbers (12 -> 18).

cases.question: What did we cover in class today?
evidence_quotes: I am not going to solve all the 25 problems on the worksheet
one or two problems on time and work also which would help you to figure out the linear equation coverage
cases.notes: GENERAL 'what did we cover' phrasing (uses 'we', not self-referential), so it stays unfiltered and widens to top_k=8. Grounded in Saisha's Math class_context (worksheet + linear-equation/time-and-work problems). Concepts taken from the live retrieved class_context, not invented.

cases.question: What did the teacher ask us to do in class?
evidence_quotes: your three minutes to go okay so what you need to do is based you base your logic here you correct your logic
i'm giving you ample time to solve seven minutes is enough time then when I say post the answer then only you post
cases.notes: GENERAL 'what did the teacher ask us to do' instruction phrasing ('the teacher', 'us' -> not self-referential), widens to top_k=8. Grounded in the teacher's class_context instructions (solve the worksheet problems, post answers when told). Concepts from live retrieved class_context.

cases.question: What determinants of supply did the class talk about?
evidence_quotes: taxes, then technology, price of related goods, it can be substitute goods
cases.notes: GENERAL topic question about class content ('the class' -> not self-referential), widens to top_k=8. Grounded in Bhagyashree's Economics class_context on the determinants of supply (taxes, technology, price of related goods / substitutes / complements). Concepts from live retrieved class_context.

cases.question: How are the determinants of supply connected to the supply function?
evidence_quotes: DRAFT - reconcile against the ingested material chunk text after running scripts.ingest_materials: the class slides/notes list the determinants of supply (e.g. taxes, technology, price of related goods) and show how each shifts the supply function / curve.
source_artifacts: materials/<Bhagyashree Economics class>/*.pptx|pdf|docx (ingested via scripts.ingest_materials as chunk_type=material)
cases.notes: The teacher-eval failure case: a 'related' question the noisy transcript alone could not answer, now expected to be answered from the class's authoritative material (materials ingestion and the materials-aware prompt). expected_chunk_types=['material'] makes this a hard regression guard: it stays RED until operators ingest this class's materials into the live store, then GREEN. required_concept_groups are deliberately relationship-level (a determinant shifts/affects the supply function) and grounded in the determinants already verified in class_context (see bhagyashree-general-determinants), not invented slide text; tighten the concept groups + evidence_quotes to the real ingested material after the live run, without loosening prompt/top_k to force a pass. A correct answer must ATTRIBUTE the material ('the class material / slides say...') with source attribution required.

cases.question: How is GDP calculated, and what are the components of national income?
cases.notes: No-world-knowledge guard: GDP / national income is a real economics concept the model KNOWS from general training but that these supply classes and their materials never covered. The bot must decline ('not enough evidence') rather than fall back to world knowledge, even after materials are ingested. Mirrors the teacher review trap question. No trust-ack required: a clean not-in-any-source refusal has no low-confidence flags to cite.

cases.question: What did the class say about complementary goods?
evidence_quotes: substitute goods, or like, what is the other name, the other one, complementary goods
cases.notes: Exact-term case (lexical-arm case): 'complementary goods' is a rare, distinctive token a single dense vector can under-rank against generic 'determinants of supply' phrasing; the lexical arm should surface it. Reuses the verified determinants class_context quote (see bhagyashree-general-determinants) — real indexed text, not invented. Unfiltered so the exact token must be found across all chunk types.

cases.question: Where did the supply schedule work out to negative 12?
evidence_quotes: Quantity supply is negative 12
cases.notes: Exact-term/number case (lexical-arm case): the exact token 'negative 12' is a strong lexical signal a dense embedder can blur into nearby numbers. Reuses Bhagyashree's verified spoken worked-example quote (see bhagyashree-negative-quantity) — real indexed text. Left unfiltered (not scoped to spoken) so retrieval must locate the exact term across all types.

cases.question: How many problems were on the workseet the teacher gave?
evidence_quotes: I am not going to solve all the 25 problems on the worksheet
cases.notes: Typo case (typo case): 'workseet' misspells 'worksheet'. Dense embeddings may absorb the typo; an exact FTS lexical arm needs trigram fuzziness to still match. Reuses Saisha's verified class_context quote (see saisha-general-what-covered) — real indexed text; the typo is only in the question, so the quote still resolves against the store.

cases.question: What are the steps for writing a thoughtful prediction about a story?
evidence_quotes: State your prediction. Use tentative language such as ‘think, perhaps, could’.
Step one is state your prediction
cases.notes: English concept case: the prediction-writing scaffold taught 2 Jul, grounded in the slide deck (material) with the teacher's spoken step-one line as a secondary anchor. Verified live 2026-07-16 (medium/high tier, grounded).

cases.question: What goes in the cue column of Cornell notes?
evidence_quotes: the cues column is where today you will just put down questions that you have
cases.notes: English concept case anchored in the teacher's own dictation (class_context) - the Cornell template docx text does not carry the word 'Cornell', so material is not the expected type here. Verified live 2026-07-16 for all three English.04 students.

cases.question: What do form, purpose and audience mean when we analyse a non-fiction text?
evidence_quotes: You can use the title and context to identify the form, purpose and audience of a text.
the people or group that the text is written for
cases.notes: English concept case: FPA definitions from the Non-fiction FPA slides (material) plus the teacher's audience definition (class_context). Verified live 2026-07-16.

cases.question: What do we need to do when analysing a quote?
evidence_quotes: What do you need to do when analysing your quote?
Define key words Provide topic sentence Unpack interesting words or phrases
cases.notes: English concept case: the quote-analysis checklist slide from 8 Jul (material). Verified live 2026-07-16 (high tier).

cases.question: Who is Helen Stoner and why is she so frightened?
evidence_quotes: her face all drawn and grey, with restless frightened eyes, like those of some hunted animal
the Roylotts of Stoke Moran
cases.notes: English concept case: character question grounded in the Speckled Band reading text (material). Avoids PDF-extraction-mangled spans (spaced letters). Verified live 2026-07-16.

cases.question: What are the determinants of supply?
cases.notes: Economics topic; Aarti is English-only (C2 cohort). Validates safe refusal + isolation on the new cohort. Verified live 2026-07-16 (low tier, deterministic refusal).

cases.question: What did we learn about Shakespeare's Macbeth?
cases.notes: Literature-adjacent trap: anshi HAS English data but Macbeth was never taught. Measured 2026-07-16: rerank 0.000 but dense 0.613 - the closest near-miss trap in the corpus. This case guards the router against any future dense-score rescue regressions.
